patch.value: GH-348: Windows nightly vitest worker fork exits unexpectedly during teardown (all 246/247 test files pass)

Nightly Validation windows-latest/Node20 fails with a single vitest 'Worker forks emitted error / Worker exited unexpectedly' unhandled error AFTER 246 of 247 test files pass (0 assertion failures). A worker process crashes during teardown, almost certainly a native resource (LanceDB or OTLP gRPC exporter) not releasing cleanly on Windows. Distinct symptom from pm-zpe7 (OTLP cleanup EBUSY) and pm-i84i (file:// TS import SyntaxError). Non-blocking for releases (auto-release green; npm 2026.6.23 published). Needs a Windows runner to reproduce/fix confidently; default vitest forks pool, no isolate/teardown override in vitest.config.ts.

value.author: codex
value.text: Continuation check (codex): GH-348 still open and remains separate from the SDK version-compatibility branch. Current main CI run 28028252104 had Windows regression (Node 20) green, while GH-348 describes intermittent Nightly Validation worker teardown after tests pass. No SDK version-compat code changes touch the suspected LanceDB/OTLP teardown surface; leave pm-2kkl open for a Windows-runner reproduction/hardening lane.

patch.value:
## Triage 2026-06-24 (still open — needs a Windows runner)

Reconfirmed scope: GH-348 is a teardown-only worker crash on windows-latest/Node20 — all 246/247 test files pass with 0 assertion failures, then a vitest fork worker exits unexpectedly. Not reproducible on Linux. The per-PR `windows-regression` CI job runs only a 4-spec subset, so it cannot reproduce a full-suite teardown crash either; only the nightly full run exercises it.

Candidate mitigations to try (ranked, each requires a Windows runner to confirm):
1. Force single-use workers for the native-resource specs: `test.poolOptions.forks.isolate` is already default; try `test.poolOptions.forks.maxForks`/`minForks` pinning or `test.fileParallelism: false` for the LanceDB/OTLP-touching specs only via a `test.projects` split, so a crashing worker is isolated.
2. Add an explicit global teardown that closes LanceDB connections and shuts down the OTLP gRPC exporter (`forceFlush()` + `shutdown()`) before the worker exits — the most likely root cause is a native handle (vectordb addon or `@opentelemetry` gRPC channel) keeping the event loop alive past teardown on Windows.
3. As a last resort, scope `dangerouslyIgnoreUnhandledErrors` narrowly — rejected for now because it would mask real teardown regressions.

Decision: not bundling a speculative vitest-config change into the TypeScript-first examples PR (pm-ax7z) — it is unverifiable on Linux and could destabilize the green Linux/macOS gates. Non-blocking for releases (auto-release green; npm 2026.6.23 published). Tracked here until a Windows runner is available to confirm option 2. Related: pm-zpe7 (OTLP EBUSY, fixed), pm-i84i (file:// TS import SyntaxError).
